# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

FROM docker:latest

RUN apk update && apk add --no-cache \
    bash \
    curl \
    jq \
    python3 \
    py3-pip \
    ca-certificates \
    openssl

RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages docker-compose

RUN addgroup -S orchestrator && adduser -S orchestrator -G orchestrator

RUN mkdir -p /app /var/log/orchestrator && \
    chown -R orchestrator:orchestrator /app /var/log/orchestrator

WORKDIR /app

COPY --chown=orchestrator:orchestrator . /app/

RUN chmod +x /app/*.sh 2>/dev/null || true

VOLUME /var/run/docker.sock

ENV DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock
ENV DOCKER_API_VERSION=1.41

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD docker info > /dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1

LABEL maintainer="orchestration-team" \
      description="Container orchestration service with Docker-in-Docker capabilities" \
      version="1.0"

# Note: This container requires the following runtime flags:
# --privileged (or specific capabilities)
# -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
# Example:
# docker run --privileged \
#   -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
#   orchestrator

CMD ["sh", "-c", "if [ -f /app/entrypoint.sh ]; then /app/entrypoint.sh; else echo 'Orchestrator running...' && tail -f /dev/null; fi"]
